title: Phase 1: Walking Skeleton — First-Release Cut, End-to-End
Project skeleton & CommandRunner seam
description:
Problem: Greenfield Go project (no go.mod, no source). Every later task invokes git/gh/claude as external processes; the fragile logic around those is why the spec chose Go. No execution seam exists yet, so nothing can be built or tested in isolation from the real binaries.

Solution: Initialise the Go module and package layout, then define a single CommandRunner interface abstracting external-command execution, with a real os/exec-backed implementation and a fake for tests. Every subsequent git/gh/claude call goes through this seam.

Outcome: go build ./... and go test ./... succeed on a clean checkout; a CommandRunner runs a command returning stdout, stderr, and exit status; the fake lets a test script any named command's result (incl. non-zero exits and command-not-found) without touching the host.

Do:
- go mod init github.com/leeovery/mint at repo root (Go 1.22+).
- Package internal/exec (or internal/runner) with CommandRunner interface: Result{Stdout, Stderr string; ExitCode int}; Run(ctx, name, args...) (Result, error); plus a stdin affordance (RunWith(ctx, stdin io.Reader, ...) or options) because Stage 4 pipes a prompt to 'claude -p' on stdin — establish the stdin path now though Phase 1 doesn't exercise AI.
- ExecRunner via os/exec.CommandContext: capture stdout/stderr separately; populate ExitCode from process exit (*exec.ExitError); distinct sentinel/typed error for binary-not-found (exec.ErrNotFound / errors.Is).
- FakeRunner: matches on command name (optionally args), returns pre-seeded Result/error, records invocation order, supports seeding command-not-found.
- Convention: non-zero exit returns a populated Result WITH a non-nil error so callers can inspect Stderr/ExitCode and branch on err.

Acceptance Criteria:
- go build ./... and go test ./... pass on clean checkout, no external binaries.
- ExecRunner.Run returns stdout/stderr separately + correct ExitCode for success and failure.
- Non-zero exit surfaced (non-nil error) with Stderr/ExitCode still readable on Result.
- Missing binary surfaced as distinguishable command-not-found, not generic failure.
- FakeRunner scripts results, records invocation order, simulates command-not-found, zero host interaction.

Tests: runs command returns stdout; captures stderr separately; surfaces non-zero exit+stderr; command-not-found distinct from non-zero exit; FakeRunner seeded results + invocation order; FakeRunner simulates command-not-found.

Edge Cases: non-zero exit captured with stderr (preflight/abort messages depend on it); command-not-found surfaced distinctly (the gh gate in 1-8 relies on telling missing apart from ran-and-failed).

Context: Settled foundation — Go chosen for testability of git/gh/claude invocation behind a single CommandRunner interface that can mock those commands. Runtime tools invoked behind the CommandRunner seam are runtime prerequisites, not build/spec deps. Stage 4 pipes the prompt to stdin and reads stdout — hence the stdin affordance now.

Spec Reference: specification.md — Settled foundations; Dependencies/Notes.

title: Minimal config load (tag_prefix, commit_prefix, release_branch, publish)
description:
Problem: The release pipeline needs a handful of [release] config values (tag_prefix, commit_prefix, release_branch, publish) to compute tags, write commit/tag subjects, gate the branch check, and decide whether to publish. Full schema and fail-loud validation are Phase 6, but Phase 1 still needs these four values loaded with correct defaults so the zero-config skeleton runs.

Solution: A minimal TOML config loader that reads .mint.toml from the repo root, extracts only the four Phase 1 keys from the [release] table, applies defaults when the file or any key is absent. No strict unknown-key/bad-type validation yet (Phase 6) — unknown keys tolerated/ignored.

Solution note: deliberately narrow slice. Do NOT implement the full schema, shared engine keys, [release.hooks], or fail-loud validation here.

Outcome: No .mint.toml -> tag_prefix=v, commit_prefix=emoji, release_branch="" (auto-derive, resolved in 1-4), publish=true. Partial file -> present keys override, absent keys default. Comments-only/blank file behaves as absent.

Do:
- Add a TOML decoder dep (github.com/pelletier/go-toml/v2 or github.com/BurntSushi/toml; pick one, go get).
- Package internal/config with a Config struct (or Release sub-struct): TagPrefix, CommitPrefix, ReleaseBranch string; Publish bool.
- Load(root string) (Config, error): read {root}/.mint.toml; if absent return defaults; decode [release] table.
- Defaults: tag_prefix->v, commit_prefix->emoji, release_branch->"" (sentinel for auto-derive), publish->true. Because publish's zero value is false, distinguish absent from explicit-false (decode into *bool then default nil->true, or pre-seed struct before decode).
- Explicit tag_prefix="" is a valid value (prefix-less tags), must NOT coerce back to v; only an ABSENT key defaults.
- Do not error on unknown keys (Phase 6 adds fail-loud); malformed-TOML may surface as an error.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Absent .mint.toml -> all four defaults (v, emoji, "", true).
- Subset of keys -> overrides those, rest at default.
- publish=false honoured (not lost to bool zero-value ambiguity).
- Explicit tag_prefix="" preserved as empty, not re-defaulted.
- Blank/comments-only file behaves exactly like absent.
- Loader testable standalone against a temp dir.

Tests: returns all defaults when absent; overrides only present keys; honours publish=false; preserves explicit empty tag_prefix; treats blank/comments file as defaults; returns configured commit_prefix and release_branch.

Edge Cases: file absent->defaults; subset present->rest default; comments/blank->defaults; publish=false vs absent (bool trap); explicit empty tag_prefix.

Context: Config fully optional, zero config = sensible defaults everywhere. Schema defaults: tag_prefix=v, commit_prefix=emoji (cosmetic), release_branch auto-derived from origin/HEAD, publish=true (false = tag+push only). Typed fail-loud validation is consolidated in Phase 6; Phase 1 loads only this subset. TOML at .mint.toml at repo root, resolved via git rev-parse --show-toplevel.

Spec Reference: specification.md — Config Format & Schema.

title: Version determination from git tags
description:
Problem: Version is sourced entirely from git tags — no file/embedded source. mint must read the complete tag set, parse only strict 3-part SemVer tags matching the configured prefix, find the global numeric maximum (not git describe's nearest-reachable), and treat no-matching-tags as 0.0.0. Without this no version can be computed and the first-release path can't start.

Solution: A version package that lists tags via CommandRunner, parses each against ^{tag_prefix}(\d+)\.(\d+)\.(\d+)$, ignores non-matching, returns the highest matching SemVer (or 0.0.0), plus a bump function computing the next version.

Outcome: CurrentVersion returns the global max matching SemVer or 0.0.0; Next(current, bump) bumps patch/minor/major. No tags: default(patch)->0.0.1, minor->0.1.0, major->1.0.0. Non-conforming tags ignored.

Do:
- Package internal/version with SemVer{Major,Minor,Patch int} and a Bump kind (patch/minor/major).
- List tags through CommandRunner (git tag --list or git for-each-ref --format '%(refname:short)' refs/tags). The fetch --tags is a preflight concern (1-6); this task reads what's present.
- Parse against exact pattern ^{tag_prefix}(\d+)\.(\d+)\.(\d+)$ (regexp.QuoteMeta the prefix). Skip non-matching: 1.2, 1.2.0-rc.1, 1.2.0.4, 1.2.0+build5, release-1.2, different/absent prefix.
- CurrentVersion(prefix): parse all matching, return numeric max by (Major,Minor,Patch); none -> 0.0.0.
- Next(current, bump): patch->Patch+1; minor->Minor+1,Patch=0; major->Major+1,Minor=0,Patch=0.
- String(prefix)/tag-format helper writing the prefix back: {prefix}{M}.{m}.{p} (used by tagging 1-10 and free-tag checks 1-5/1-6).
- Numeric (not lexical) comparison: v10.0.0>v9.0.0, v1.10.0>v1.9.0.

Acceptance Criteria:
- No matching tags -> 0.0.0.
- No tags: default->0.0.1, minor->0.1.0, major->1.0.0.
- Non-conforming tags (1.2, 1.2.0-rc.1, 1.2.0.4, 1.2.0+build5, release-1.2) ignored.
- Non-matching prefix ignored; only {tag_prefix}... count; empty prefix matches bare X.Y.Z.
- Latest = global numeric maximum, double-digit segments numeric (v10.0.0>v9.0.0).
- Tag formatting writes the configured prefix back (v0.0.1, or 0.0.1 with empty prefix).

Tests: returns 0.0.0 when no tags; 0.0.0 when none match prefix; ignores non-3-part-semver (rc/4-seg/build/release-1.2); global numeric max not lexical; ignores other prefixes, matches configured; matches bare X.Y.Z when prefix empty; bumps patch/minor/major incl from 0.0.0; formats version back with prefix.

Edge Cases: no matching tags->0.0.0; non-matching ignored (1.2, 1.2.0-rc.1, 1.2.0.4, release-1.2); mixed prefixes (only configured counts); double-digit segments numeric not lexical.

Context: Current version is highest SemVer tag (prefix-stripped); no file/embedded source; none->0.0.0. Latest = numerically highest matching globally, not git describe. Strict SemVer 2.0.0, 3 numeric segments only; anything else ignored. Pattern ^{tag_prefix}(\d+)\.(\d+)\.(\d+)$. tag_prefix default v, overridable to "". First release: no tags->0.0.0 so release->0.0.1, -m->0.1.0, -M->1.0.0. --set-version and explicit-version validation deferred to Phase 4 — do not implement here.

Spec Reference: specification.md — Stage 1 Version Determination & Tag Grammar.

title: Repo root anchoring & release-branch resolution
description:
Problem: mint must anchor every operation at the repo root and know the release branch before running preflight gates. Root via git rev-parse --show-toplevel; release branch defaults to one auto-derived from origin/HEAD (resolves main/master with zero config) unless overridden by config. Without these, config loading (reads .mint.toml at root) and the branch gate (1-5) have no anchor.

Solution: A repository-context resolver using CommandRunner to find the repo root (failing cleanly if not a git repo) and determine the release branch — config override if set, else the branch origin/HEAD points at.

Outcome: ResolveRoot returns absolute repo root or a clean 'not a git repository' abort. ResolveReleaseBranch returns configured release_branch if non-empty, else the branch derived from origin/HEAD (e.g. main), surfacing a clear condition when origin/HEAD unset and no override.

Do:
- Package internal/gitrepo (or internal/repo): ResolveRoot(runner) runs git rev-parse --show-toplevel; success->trimmed path; failure->clean error suitable for an abort message, not a panic.
- Code comment recording spec resolution semantics: --show-toplevel resolves to innermost enclosing repo or linked worktree; submodule is its own repo, linked worktree shares main repo's ref store. No special handling required — git is authoritative — comment records intent.
- ResolveReleaseBranch(runner, cfg): if cfg.ReleaseBranch != "" return it; else derive from origin/HEAD (git symbolic-ref --short refs/remotes/origin/HEAD -> origin/main, strip origin/; or git rev-parse --abbrev-ref origin/HEAD). Return short branch name (main/master).
- origin/HEAD unset: if derivation fails and no override, return a distinguishable error/condition. Phase 1 may surface as abort; do NOT auto-pick main silently.
- Wire the config-loaded release_branch (1-2) as override source.

Acceptance Criteria:
- ResolveRoot returns repo root via git rev-parse --show-toplevel.
- Outside a git repo, ResolveRoot aborts cleanly (no panic).
- ResolveReleaseBranch returns config release_branch when set, ignoring origin/HEAD.
- No override -> derives from origin/HEAD (e.g. main).
- origin/HEAD unset and no override -> clear distinguishable condition, no silent default.
- All git calls via CommandRunner, exercised with FakeRunner.

Tests: resolves repo root; aborts cleanly when not a git repo; returns configured release_branch when set; derives from origin/HEAD when no override; surfaces clear condition when origin/HEAD unset and no override.

Edge Cases: not a git repo->clean abort; origin/HEAD unset->distinguishable condition (no silent default); release_branch override->used verbatim, derivation skipped.

Context: Gate 1 — git repo present, anchored at repo root via git rev-parse --show-toplevel. Submodule/worktree resolution: innermost enclosing repo or linked worktree. Gate 2 — on the release branch, default-on, auto-derived from origin/HEAD, override via release_branch. Config location resolved via show-toplevel. --any-branch escape hatch deferred to Phase 4.

Spec Reference: specification.md — Stage 2 Preflight & Safety Gates (gates 1-2); Config Format & Schema/Location.
